title: plugins.yaml
description: Plugin manifest. Each entry groups skills under a domain plugin with per-product manifest paths, dependencies, and install policy.

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. Drift audit uses this to pick the validator.

plugins.description: List of plugin entries, one per domain plugin.

id.description: Plugin identifier, normally equal to the owning domain (`<domain>`).

domain.description: Domain this plugin owns. Cross-references skill `domain` fields in `skills.yaml`.

contained_skills.description: Canonical skill IDs (`<domain>.<skill>`) contained in this plugin.

product_manifests.description: Per-product manifest paths. Both Codex `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` and Claude `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` are loaded by their product's plugin loader at runtime (the kit ships a marketplace for each). Codex discovers each plugin's bundled `skills/<skill>/SKILL.md` directly and ignores the manifest `skills` array, which is kept as source-organisation audit metadata (issue #435 supersedes Resolved Decision #10; issue #437 cut over Codex to default plugin activation).

hermes.description: Path to `.hermes-plugin/plugin.json` under `targets/hermes/`. Metadata-only; Hermes discovers skills from the filesystem and does not load `plugin.json` at runtime.

dependencies.description: Other plugin IDs this plugin depends on.

install_policy.description: How install handles this plugin. `always` installs on every host; `opt-in` requires explicit profile / flag; `experimental` warns on install.
